description: M6: WebSocket & Real-Time — Session-level WebSocket connection with presence tracking, chat/board broadcasting, offline handling, and multi-user collaboration awareness.

userStories.title: Django Channels WebSocket consumer — connect, token auth, disconnect, error handling
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want a Django Channels WebSocket consumer that validates tokens on handshake and manages connection lifecycle so that authenticated users can establish secure real-time connections.
acceptanceCriteria: WebSocket consumer accepts connections at /ws/?token=<jwt>
Token extracted from query parameter and validated on handshake
Invalid/missing/expired tokens reject connection with 403
On successful connect, consumer stores user_id and connection ID in consumer scope
On disconnect, consumer cleans up presence and channel group subscriptions
Unexpected errors log to console and send error message to client before closing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § WebSocket Protocol — handshake URL format, auth on connect, message envelope
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Connection Lifecycle — connect, validate, disconnect flow
Files: services/gateway/apps/websocket/consumers.py (new), services/gateway/apps/websocket/routing.py, services/gateway/apps/websocket/middleware.py (new for token validation)
Gotchas: Token validation must use the same middleware/utilities as REST endpoints (check services/gateway/middleware/ for existing auth patterns). Django Channels consumers don't have request.user automatically — you must populate it from token validation in middleware or in consumer.connect().

api_endpoints: WebSocket /ws/?token=<jwt> — session-level connection, token auth on handshake
data_model: users: id uuid PK, email, display_name — user lookup from token

userStories.title: Channel group management — subscribe_idea, unsubscribe_idea, group join/leave
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want the consumer to handle subscribe_idea and unsubscribe_idea messages so that clients receive events only for ideas they're viewing.
acceptanceCriteria: Consumer accepts {"type": "subscribe_idea", "idea_id": "<uuid>"} from client
On subscribe_idea: add consumer's channel to idea's group (channel layer group_add)
On subscribe_idea: validate user has access to idea (owner, co-owner, or collaborator)
If access denied, send error message to client and do not add to group
Consumer accepts {"type": "unsubscribe_idea", "idea_id": "<uuid>"}
On unsubscribe_idea: remove consumer from group (channel layer group_discard)
On disconnect: automatically unsubscribe from all active groups
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § WebSocket Protocol — subscribe_idea / unsubscribe_idea client events
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Real-Time Transport Design — channel groups per idea
Files: services/gateway/apps/websocket/consumers.py
Gotchas: Channel group naming convention should be "idea_<uuid>". Use self.channel_layer.group_add() and group_discard(). Track subscribed ideas in consumer instance state so disconnect can clean up all groups. Access validation: query idea_collaborators table + check owner_id/co_owner_id.

api_endpoints: subscribe_idea: {type, idea_id} — add client to idea group
unsubscribe_idea: {type, idea_id} — remove client from idea group

userStories.title: Chat message broadcast — on REST POST success, broadcast chat.message.created to idea group
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want chat message creation to broadcast via WebSocket so that all users on the idea see new messages in real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: Gateway event consumer listens for 'chat.message.created' broker event
On event: extract idea_id and message payload
Broadcast {"type": "chat_message", "idea_id": "<uuid>", "payload": {...}} to idea group
Payload includes: id, sender_type, sender {id, display_name}, ai_agent, content, message_type, created_at
Broadcast uses channel_layer.group_send() to "idea_<uuid>" group
Consumer's receive method forwards group_send messages to WebSocket clients
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § WebSocket Protocol — chat_message event schema
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Board Sync Protocol — REST mutation → persist → WebSocket broadcast
Files: services/gateway/events/consumers.py (check if exists, or create chat_consumer.py), services/gateway/apps/websocket/consumers.py (add receive handler for group messages)
Gotchas: The REST endpoint (POST /api/ideas/:id/chat) already exists from M3. It publishes a broker event after persisting. You need to consume that event and translate it to a WebSocket broadcast. Check services/gateway/events/ for existing event consumer patterns.

data_model: chat_messages: id, sender_type, sender_id, content, created_at

userStories.title: Board sync — broadcast board.node.updated / board.connection.updated after REST persistence
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want board mutations to broadcast via WebSocket so that all users see board changes in real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: Event consumer listens for 'board.node.created', 'board.node.updated', 'board.node.deleted' events
Event consumer listens for 'board.connection.created', 'board.connection.updated', 'board.connection.deleted' events
On event: batch related changes into a single board_update broadcast
Broadcast {"type": "board_update", "idea_id": "<uuid>", "payload": {nodes_created, nodes_updated, nodes_deleted, connections_created, connections_updated, connections_deleted, source}} to idea group
Payload source field = 'user' or 'ai' depending on event metadata
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § WebSocket Protocol — board_update event schema
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Board Sync Protocol — hybrid model, REST-first then broadcast
Files: services/gateway/events/board_consumer.py (likely exists from M4/M5, extend it), services/gateway/apps/websocket/consumers.py
Gotchas: The board REST endpoints (POST/PATCH/DELETE on nodes and connections) already exist from M4/M5. They should already be publishing broker events. Check services/gateway/events/board_consumer.py for existing consumption. You're adding WebSocket broadcast as a side effect. Batching: if multiple events arrive in quick succession, debounce or batch them into one board_update message.

data_model: board_nodes: id, node_type, title, body, position_x, position_y, is_locked, created_by

userStories.title: Board awareness events — selection/deselection, lock state changes (WebSocket-only, no persistence)
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want ephemeral board awareness events (selection, lock changes) to broadcast without persisting so that users see what others are doing on the board.
acceptanceCriteria: Consumer accepts {"type": "board_selection", "idea_id": "<uuid>", "payload": {node_id, user}} from client
On board_selection: immediately broadcast to idea group (no persistence)
Broadcast echoes selection to all subscribers except sender
Consumer accepts board_lock_change events (when lock toggled via REST, broadcast via existing board_consumer)
Lock change broadcasts {"type": "board_lock_change", "idea_id": "<uuid>", "payload": {node_id, is_locked, changed_by}}
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § WebSocket Protocol — board_selection (client→server, server→client), board_lock_change (server→client only)
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Board Sync Protocol — awareness events (selections, lock state) are WebSocket-only, ephemeral
Files: services/gateway/apps/websocket/consumers.py, services/gateway/events/board_consumer.py
Gotchas: Selection events come from client, not from REST. The consumer receives the message and broadcasts it. Lock changes are triggered by PATCH REST calls that persist the lock state — those publish events that get broadcast (already handled in US-004, but verify lock-specific broadcast).

api_endpoints: board_selection client event: {type, idea_id, payload: {node_id, user}}
PATCH /api/ideas/:id/board/nodes/:id — lock toggle triggers board_lock_change event

userStories.title: Frontend WebSocket connection manager — connect on auth, reconnect with exponential backoff, session-level
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want a WebSocket connection manager that establishes a session-level connection and handles reconnection so that users have a stable real-time connection.
acceptanceCriteria: Connection manager module/hook connects to /ws/?token=<jwt> on app mount (after auth)
Token retrieved from auth context/localStorage
On connection open: dispatch Redux action to set connection state = 'online'
On connection close: dispatch state = 'offline', start reconnection with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, ..., max 30s configurable)
On connection error: log error, trigger reconnection
Reconnection stops if user logs out or navigates away
Cleanup: on unmount, close connection and clear reconnection timers
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: connection established, visible in DevTools Network → WS tab
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Connection Lifecycle — connect on load, reconnect with exponential backoff, max 30s
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 14. Connection State Indicator — green/red dot + Online/Offline label
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/runtime-safety.md § LOOP-004: WebSocket Reconnection Backoff — backoff cap, cleanup on unmount
Files: frontend/src/hooks/use-websocket.ts (new), frontend/src/store/websocket-slice.ts (new Redux slice for connection state), frontend/src/app/providers.tsx (mount WebSocket provider)
Gotchas: The connection is session-level (not per-idea). Use a React context or provider to manage the connection globally. Reconnection backoff must cap at max_reconnection_backoff from admin parameters (default 30s). Cleanup timers on unmount to prevent memory leaks (see LOOP-004 runtime safety tests).

test_cases: LOOP-004 | Runtime | WebSocket reconnection backoff cap + cleanup | Disconnect, never reconnect | Backoff caps at 30s, timers cleared on unmount

userStories.title: Presence tracking — online/idle/offline states, stacked avatars in workspace header, multi-tab dedup
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want presence indicators showing who's on the idea so that users see who they're collaborating with.
acceptanceCriteria: On subscribe_idea, send {"type": "presence_update", "payload": {state: "active", idea_id}} to server
Server broadcasts presence updates to idea group
Redux slice tracks presence: { user_id, display_name, state: online|idle|offline }[]
PresenceIndicators component in workspace header shows stacked avatars (max 4 visible, +N overflow)
Idle avatars: opacity-50, grayscale filter
Multi-tab dedup: same user in multiple tabs shows as single presence (server-side dedup by user_id)
On unsubscribe_idea or disconnect, server removes user from presence list and broadcasts update
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: open idea in 2 tabs with different dev users, see 2 avatars; open same user in 2 tabs, see 1 avatar
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § WebSocket Protocol — presence_update client event, presence_update server event
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 9.2 Stacked Avatars (Presence) — max 4 visible, overlap by 8px, +N overflow, idle = opacity-50 + grayscale
Design: docs/03-design/component-inventory.md § Collaboration — PresenceIndicators (Workspace header)
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-6.3.x — presence states, multi-tab dedup, idle detection
Files: frontend/src/store/websocket-slice.ts (add presence state), frontend/src/components/workspace/PresenceIndicators.tsx (new), frontend/src/components/workspace/WorkspaceHeader.tsx (integrate PresenceIndicators), services/gateway/apps/websocket/consumers.py (add presence tracking logic server-side)
Gotchas: Server-side dedup: track presence per user_id (not per channel). When user connects from multiple tabs, presence list shows them once. Idle state: client sends presence_update with state='idle' after idle_timeout (default 5 min). This is a future enhancement for M16 — for now, just implement online/offline. The test matrix includes idle, but the milestone scope says 'partial' idle detection (F-15.1).

api_endpoints: presence_update server: {type, idea_id, payload: {user: {id, display_name}, state}}
data_model: users: id, display_name — presence user info
test_cases: T-6.3.01 | Integration | Online user shows in presence list | User subscribes to idea | Avatar appears
T-6.3.02 | Integration | Multi-tab dedup | Same user in 2 tabs | Single presence entry

userStories.title: User selection highlights on board — colored borders with username when another user selects a node
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want selection highlights showing which nodes other users are editing so that users avoid edit conflicts.
acceptanceCriteria: On node selection (ReactFlow onNodeClick), send {"type": "board_selection", "idea_id": "<uuid>", "payload": {node_id, user}} to server
On deselection, send board_selection with node_id = null
Redux slice tracks selections: { user_id, display_name, node_id }[]
Custom node components (BoxNode, GroupNode, FreeTextNode) check if another user selected the node
If selected by other user: render 2px colored border + small name label above node
Each user gets a unique color from a predefined palette (cycle through colors if >palette size)
Own selections don't show the highlight (only other users' selections)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: open idea in 2 tabs with different dev users, select a node in tab 1, see colored border + name in tab 2
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § WebSocket Protocol — board_selection client→server, server→client
Design: docs/03-design/component-inventory.md § Board — UserSelectionHighlight
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 6.1 Box Node — 'Selected by other user: border-2 in user's color, small name label above node'
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-3.5.x — selection highlights, multi-user board editing
Files: frontend/src/store/websocket-slice.ts (add selections state), frontend/src/components/board/BoxNode.tsx (add selection highlight), frontend/src/components/board/GroupNode.tsx, frontend/src/components/board/FreeTextNode.tsx, frontend/src/components/board/BoardCanvas.tsx (send selection events on onNodeClick), frontend/src/hooks/use-websocket.ts (handle incoming board_selection messages)
Gotchas: Color assignment: use a deterministic mapping (e.g., user_id hash % palette.length) so the same user gets the same color consistently. Don't show highlight for own selections. Clear selection when user disconnects.

test_cases: T-3.5.01 | Integration | Selection highlights visible | User A selects node | User B sees border + name
existing_code: frontend/src/components/board/BoxNode.tsx — extend with selection highlight rendering

userStories.title: Offline banner + behavior — 'Currently offline' with countdown, chat/board locked, reconnect button
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want an offline banner that appears when the connection drops so that users understand they're offline and can reconnect.
acceptanceCriteria: OfflineBanner component renders when connection state = 'offline'
Banner shows: 'Currently offline. Retrying in X seconds' with countdown matching reconnection backoff
Manual 'Reconnect Now' button triggers immediate reconnection attempt
While offline: chat input disabled with overlay message, board read-only (no drag/edit)
On reconnect success: banner disappears, chat/board unlock, trigger REST fetch to sync latest state
Banner positioned at top of workspace, full-width, red background with white text
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: kill server, see offline banner with countdown; restart server, click Reconnect, banner disappears
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Connection Lifecycle — offline banner, reconnect with exponential backoff
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 11.5 Banners — full-width, semantic color, slide-down animation
Design: docs/03-design/component-inventory.md § Common — OfflineBanner (Workspace)
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-6.2.01, T-6.5.x — offline banner, locked state
Files: frontend/src/components/common/OfflineBanner.tsx (new), frontend/src/pages/IdeaWorkspace/index.tsx (integrate banner), frontend/src/components/chat/ChatInput.tsx (add offline overlay), frontend/src/components/board/BoardCanvas.tsx (disable interactions when offline)
Gotchas: Countdown synchronization: the banner's countdown must match the actual reconnection timer. Use shared state from websocket slice. LockOverlay pattern: reuse the pattern from chat rate limit lockout (F-2.11, see component-specs § 5.8). State sync on reconnect: after successful reconnection, refetch chat messages and board state to get any updates that happened while offline.

test_cases: T-6.2.01 | Unit | Offline banner appears on disconnect | connection state = offline | Banner visible
T-6.5.01 | Integration | Chat locked when offline | Disconnect | Chat input disabled, overlay shown

userStories.title: Connection state indicator — navbar green/red dot + Online/Offline label
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want a connection indicator in the navbar so that users always know their connection status.
acceptanceCriteria: ConnectionIndicator component in navbar (right side, near user dropdown)
Shows green dot + 'Online' when connection state = 'online'
Shows red dot + 'Offline' when connection state = 'offline'
Dot: 8px circle, label: Gotham Book text-xs, gap-1.5
Label hidden on small screens (dot only)
Color transition: 200ms crossfade when state changes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: connection indicator shows 'Online' with green dot; kill server, indicator changes to 'Offline' with red dot
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Connection Lifecycle — connection state indicator in navbar
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 14. Connection State Indicator — green/red dot + Online/Offline label
Design: docs/03-design/component-inventory.md § Layout — ConnectionIndicator (Navbar)
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-6.6.01 — connection indicator displays correct state
Files: frontend/src/components/layout/ConnectionIndicator.tsx (new), frontend/src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx (integrate ConnectionIndicator)
Gotchas: Connection state comes from websocket-slice Redux state. The indicator is a passive display component, no interactive behavior. Position in navbar: right side, before NotificationBell and UserDropdown.

test_cases: T-6.6.01 | Unit | Connection indicator displays correct state | connection state = online/offline | Green 'Online' or Red 'Offline' displayed
